description_long: This object consists of three distinct grave markers arranged side by side. The central marker is a large, ornate cross with a circular halo, mounted on a rectangular pedestal, and features a greenish patina along its edges, suggesting weathering or moss. Flanking the cross are two simpler headstones, each with a slightly different top profile—one is arched and the other is angled. All three markers are made of dark stone, likely granite or similar, with subtle engravings or reliefs visible on their faces. The surfaces are matte and rough, with green highlights accentuating the tops and edges, giving a sense of age and exposure to the elements. The overall grouping evokes a small, traditional graveyard plot, suitable for placement in a park, churchyard, or urban cemetery.
description: A trio of weathered stone grave markers, including a large cross and two headstones, with green patina and rough, aged surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three grave markers: a central cross with a circular halo and two flanking headstones with visible engravings.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the cross and headstones, with minimal detail visible except the depth of the structures.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the grave markers, highlighting their simple, unadorned surfaces and the green patina along the tops.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thickness and layered construction of the cross and headstones.
